title: 7 tips for using Antigravity 2.0 on enterprise codebases, coding phase
summary: Martin Omander presents seven practical tactics for deploying Antigravity 2.0 agents on large enterprise codebases. The approaches cover encoding team standards into agent skills, automating test-and-fix loops, using browser automation for UI validation, async task execution, architecture visualization, and pre-submission code verification.
writeup: Martin Omander walks through seven tactics for running Antigravity 2.0 agents against large enterprise codebases during the coding phase. The tactics include encoding team standards into agent skills, automating test-and-fix loops, validating UI through browser automation, executing tasks asynchronously, visualizing architecture, and verifying code before submission. For practitioners already working with agentic workflows, the value is concrete: each tactic targets a recurring friction point in applying code-generation agents to codebases too large for a single context, and the test-and-fix and pre-submission verification steps push correctness checks into the loop rather than leaving them to manual review.
